visa_types.documents_required:
Document Checklist – Click here
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries.
visa_types.visa_fees:
[svk > tur > en > Table:Cultural / Scientific / Educational / Internships - Training / Sports / Medical / Treatment > Visa Fees 1]
The above-mentioned Visa Fee is to be paid in Cash or by Debit/Credit Card.
VFS Service charge:
Applicants applying at the Visa Application Centre shall be levied a service charge of 40 Euros (inclusive of Goods and Services Tax), payable online only at the time of appointment booking
Please Note:
- The child between 0 - 12 years on the date of submission at VFS are Biometric exempt.
- The applicable visa fee in Euro is as per the current exchange rate. The same is subject to change without notice.
- The Visa fee and service charges are non-refundable in case the visa is refused.
visa_types.processing_time: From the date of receipt of application at the Embassy of Slovakia, it takes 15 working days to process the visa application. The duration of days when the application is in transit between the Visa Application Centre and the Embassy are not counted.

Application for Visa
To Download a Form:

- When the form opens, save the form on your computer.
OR

- Save the form on your computer.
Application forms can also be found here (https://www.mzv.sk/en/web/ankara-en/visa-and-services/visa-application-forms).
visa_types.overview:
Overview
The Cultural, Sports or Religious Events and Film Crews visa category is designed for applicants who wish to travel to Slovakia (or any Schengen country) to participate in official events, performances, competitions, festivals, ceremonies, or filming activities. This is a short‑stay Schengen Visa (Type C) allowing visits of up to 90 days within a 180‑day period.
Please visit https://www.mzv.sk/web/ankara-en (https://www.mzv.sk/web/ankara-en) to find out more.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
The Cultural, Sports or Religious Events and Film Crews visa category is designed for applicants who wish to travel to Slovakia (or any Schengen country) to participate in official events, performances, competitions, festivals, ceremonies, or filming activities. This is a short‑stay Schengen Visa (Type C) allowing visits of up to 90 days within a 180‑day period.
Please visit https://www.mzv.sk/web/ankara-en (https://www.mzv.sk/web/ankara-en) to find out more. 

## Visa Fees

[svk > tur > en > Table:Cultural / Scientific / Educational / Internships - Training / Sports / Medical / Treatment > Visa Fees 1]
The above-mentioned Visa Fee is to be paid in Cash or by Debit/Credit Card.
VFS Service charge:
Applicants applying at the Visa Application Centre shall be levied a service charge of 40 Euros (inclusive of Goods and Services Tax), payable online only at the time of appointment booking
Please Note:
- The child between 0 - 12 years on the date of submission at VFS are Biometric exempt.
- The applicable visa fee in Euro is as per the current exchange rate. The same is subject to change without notice.
- The Visa fee and service charges are non-refundable in case the visa is refused.

## Documents Required
Document Checklist – Click here
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries.

## Photo Specifications
Please provide one recent colour photograph (not more than 6 months old) of yourself, meeting the following requirements:
- Taken against a light (white or off-white) background so that features are distinguishable and contrast against the background.
- Clear quality and with the face in focus
- Full face: non-smiling, no sunglasses, no hat/cap or any head covering, unless the applicant wears such items because of his/her religious belief or ethnic background
- Printed on normal photographic paper (camera print)
- Please stick the photograph on the Visa Application Form
If the photographs presented do not meet these requirements (click here), then the visa application will be considered incomplete and applicant will be asked to take new photographs in the photo booth in the visa application centre. 

## Processing Time
From the date of receipt of application at the Embassy of Slovakia, it takes 15 working days to process the visa application. The duration of days when the application is in transit between the Visa Application Centre and the Embassy are not counted.

## Download Forms
Application for Visa
To Download a Form: 
- Click on the form link
- When the form opens, save the form on your computer.
OR
- Press the right button on your mouse on the required link.
- Select Save Target As from the list
- Save the form on your computer.
Application forms can also be found here (https://www.mzv.sk/en/web/ankara-en/visa-and-services/visa-application-forms).

visa_types.overview:
Overview
This category of visa caters to people wishing to visit Slovakia for Spuse /Child / Parent of a Slovak Citizen purposes. Applicants will have to provide evidence that he/she falls under this category.
Please visit https://www.mzv.sk/web/ankara-en (https://www.mzv.sk/web/ankara-en) to find out more.

visa_types.name: Territorial Jurisdiction
visa_types.overview:
Applicants are kindly reminded that visa applications must be submitted at the designated application centers according to their place of residence. Applications submitted outside the appropriate jurisdiction may not be accepted.
*ANKARA & ANTALYA Jurisdiction*
Applicants residing in the following provinces must submit their applications in Ankara or Antalya:
Adana, Adıyaman, Afyonkarahisar, Ağrı, Aksaray, Amasya, Ankara, Antalya, Ardahan, Artvin, Batman, Bayburt, Bingöl, Bitlis, Burdur, Çankırı, Çorum, Diyarbakır, Elazığ, Erzincan, Erzurum, Eskişehir, Gaziantep, Giresun, Gümüşhane, Hakkari, Hatay, Iğdır, Isparta, Kahramanmaraş, Karaman, Kars, Kastamonu, Kayseri, Kırıkkale, Kırşehir, Kilis, Konya, Malatya, Mardin, Mersin, Muş, Nevşehir, Niğde, Ordu, Osmaniye, Rize, Samsun, Siirt, Sinop, Sivas, Şanlıurfa, Şırnak, Tokat, Trabzon, Tunceli, Van, Yozgat

*ISTANBUL & IZMIR Jurisdiction*
Applicants residing in the following provinces must submit their applications in Istanbul or Izmir:
Aydın, Balıkesir, Bartın, Bilecik, Bolu, Bursa, Çanakkale, Denizli, Düzce, Edirne, İstanbul, İzmir, Karabük, Kırklareli, Kocaeli, Kütahya, Manisa, Muğla, Sakarya, Tekirdağ, Uşak, Yalova, Zonguldak

visa_types.overview:
Overview
The Truck Driver visa category is intended for professional drivers who need to travel to Slovakia as part of their international transport duties. This visa supports drivers engaged in the movement of goods across borders on behalf of transportation or logistics companies.
Please visit https://www.mzv.sk/web/ankara-en (https://www.mzv.sk/web/ankara-en) to find out more.

visa_types.name: Long Term Visa (National Visa)
visa_types.overview:
A Long‑Term Visa, also known as a National Visa (Type D), allows a foreign national to stay in Slovaki for more than 90 days for a specific purpose.
Please click here to see important notice for national visas.
visa_types.full_text:

visa_types.overview:
Overview
The Visiting Family/Friend category for Slovakia applies to travelers who want to visit a family member or friend legally residing in Slovakia. This is a short‑stay Schengen visa (Type C), allowing stays of up to 90 days within a 180‑day period.
Please visit https://www.mzv.sk/web/ankara-en (https://www.mzv.sk/web/ankara-en) to find out more.

A Long‑Term Employment Visa (National Visa – Type D) is issued to applicants who have secured a job offer or employment contract in Slovakia. This visa is intended for stays longer than 90 days

visa_types.overview:
Overview
The Business visa for Slovakia is a short‑stay Schengen Visa issued to applicants traveling for official, commercial, or professional purposes. It allows stays of up to 90 days within a 180 day period and is intended for individuals attending meetings, conferences, negotiations, or business‑related events.
Please visit https://www.mzv.sk/web/ankara-en (https://www.mzv.sk/web/ankara-en) to find out more.
visa_types.full_text:

Please click here to download national visa form
Application forms can also be found here.
visa_types.overview:
Overview
A Long‑Term Study Visa (National Visa – Type D) is issued to applicants who intend to stay in Slovakia for more than 90 days for educational purposes. This visa is required for students who have been accepted into a university program, long-term course, research project, or exchange program.
Please visit https://www.mzv.sk/web/ankara-en (https://www.mzv.sk/web/ankara-en) to find out more.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
A Long‑Term Study Visa (National Visa – Type D) is issued to applicants who intend to stay in Slovakia for more than 90 days for educational purposes. This visa is required for students who have been accepted into a university program, long-term course, research project, or exchange program.
Please visit https://www.mzv.sk/web/ankara-en (https://www.mzv.sk/web/ankara-en) to find out more. 

## Visa Fees

[svk > tur > en > Table:Long term Visa  > Visa Fees 1]
The above-mentioned Visa Fee is to be paid in Cash or by Debit/Credit Card.
VFS Service charge:
Applicants applying at the Visa Application Centre shall be levied a service charge of 40 Euros (inclusive of Goods and Services Tax), payable online only at the time of appointment booking
Please Note: 
- The applicable visa fee in Euro is as per the current exchange rate. The same is subject to change without notice.
- The Visa fee and service charges are non-refundable in case the visa is refused.

## Documents Required
Please click here to see checklist.

## Photo Specifications
Please provide one recent colour photograph (not more than 6 months old) of yourself, meeting the following requirements:
- Taken against a light (white or off-white) background so that features are distinguishable and contrast against the background.
- Clear quality and with the face in focus
- Full face: non-smiling, no sunglasses, no hat/cap or any head covering, unless the applicant wears such items because of his/her religious belief or ethnic background
- Printed on normal photographic paper (camera print)
- Please stick the photograph on the Visa Application Form
If the photographs presented do not meet these requirements (click here), then the visa application will be considered incomplete and applicant will be asked to take new photographs in the photo booth in the visa application centre. 

## Processing Time
From the date of receipt of application at the Embassy of Slovakia, it takes 30 working days to process the visa application. The duration of days when the application is in transit between the Visa Application Centre and the Embassy are not counted.

## Download Forms
Please click here to download national visa form
Application forms can also be found here.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
This category of visa is designed for individuals who intend to visit Slovakia for tourism-related purposes. Applicants must provide supporting documentation demonstrating that their primary reason for travel is tourism.
Please visit https://www.mzv.sk/web/ankara-en (https://www.mzv.sk/web/ankara-en) to find out more.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
This category of visa is designed for individuals who intend to visit Slovakia for tourism-related purposes. Applicants must provide supporting documentation demonstrating that their primary reason for travel is tourism.
Please visit https://www.mzv.sk/web/ankara-en (https://www.mzv.sk/web/ankara-en) to find out more.

## Visa Fees

[svk > tur > en > Table:Tourism > Visa Fees 1]
The above-mentioned Visa Fee is to be paid in Cash or by Debit/Credit Card.
VFS Service charge:
Applicants applying at the Visa Application Centre shall be levied a service charge of 40 Euros (inclusive of Goods and Services Tax), payable online only at the time of appointment booking.
Please Note:
- The child between 0 - 12 years on the date of submission at VFS are Biometric exempt.
- The applicable visa fee in Euro is as per the current exchange rate. The same is subject to change without notice.
- The Visa fee and service charges are non-refundable in case the visa is refused.

## Documents Required
Document Checklist – Click here
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries.

## Photo Specifications
Please provide one recent colour photograph (not more than 6 months old) of yourself, meeting the following requirements:
- Taken against a light (white or off-white) background so that features are distinguishable and contrast against the background.
- Clear quality and with the face in focus
- Full face: non-smiling, no sunglasses, no hat/cap or any head covering, unless the applicant wears such items because of his/her religious belief or ethnic background
- Printed on normal photographic paper (camera print)
- Please stick the photograph on the Visa Application Form
If the photographs presented do not meet these requirements (click here), then the visa application will be considered incomplete and applicant will be asked to take new photographs in the photo booth in the visa application centre. 

## Processing Time
From the date of receipt of application at the Embassy of Slovakia, it takes 15 working days to process the visa application. The duration of days when the application is in transit between the Visa Application Centre and the Embassy are not counted.

## Download Forms
Application for Visa
To Download a Form: 
- Click on the form link
- When the form opens, save the form on your computer.
OR
- Press the right button on your mouse on the required link.
- Select Save Target As from the list
- Save the form on your computer.
Application forms can also be found here (https://www.mzv.sk/en/web/ankara-en/visa-and-services/visa-application-forms).
